title: 10 possible side effects of Silybum marianum (Milk Thistle) if used improperly
intro: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. When used improperly, it can cause several health issues. First, consuming excessive amounts of milk thistle can lead to digestive problems such as diarrhea and nausea. Second, it can cause allergic reactions, particularly in those who are allergic to plants in the daisy family. Third, milk thistle can interact with certain medications, including ones used for blood pressure and diabetes, which can lead to dangerous complications. Fourth, it can lower blood sugar levels, which can be problematic for people with diabetes. Lastly, pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid using milk thistle as its safety and effects on fetal development or infant health are unknown. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.

side_effects.side_effect_name: Swelling of the mouth and tongue
